# Figure description: Картограмма_геологической_изученности.jpg

---

# Figure Description: Cartogram of Geological Survey Coverage

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index/cartogram map** (картограмма геологической изученности) — specifically a **geological survey coverage map** at 1:1,000,000 scale. It shows the spatial distribution and types of geological survey work completed across a region, organized by numbered map sheet blocks. This type of figure is standard in Soviet-era geological reporting to document what survey work has been conducted and at what scales across different sub-areas.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a large region in **northern Kazakhstan**, corresponding to international map sheet rows **N-42** (northern row, labeled "N M" on both left and right margins) and the broader survey area of the **Западно-Тенизская партия (Zapadno-Tenizskaya / West-Teniz Party)**. The area encompasses sheets labeled with Roman numerals and combinations:

- Upper row: **XXXI, XXXII, XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV**
- Middle rows: **I, II, III, IV, V**
- Lower-middle rows: **VII, VIII, IX, X, XI**
- Bottom rows: **XIII, XIV, XV, XVI, XVII**

Each block is subdivided into smaller quadrangles corresponding to 1:200,000 and larger-scale survey sheets. The map covers approximately the full extent of map sheets **M-42** and **N-42** at the 1:1,000,000 overview scale.

---

## Key Content

The cartogram displays the **extent and scale of geological surveys** completed across each map sheet block, using different line styles to distinguish survey scales:

**Legend (Условные обозначения) specifies four survey types:**
1. **Solid thick line** — Съёмки м-ба 1:200,000 (surveys at 1:200,000 scale)
2. **Dashed line** — Съёмки м-ба 1:50,000 (surveys at 1:50,000 scale)
3. **Dotted line** — Съёмки м-ба 1:10,000–2,000 (detailed surveys at 1:10,000 to 1:2,000 scale)
4. **Shaded/hatched areas** — Площадь геологического доизучения м-ба 1:200,000 Западно-Тенизской партии (area of geological re-study/supplementary survey at 1:200,000 by the West-Teniz Party — this defines the primary study area of the current report)

The **bold outlined irregular polygon** (thick solid boundary) running through the center and lower portions of the map delineates the **specific work area of the West-Teniz Party** (the subject of Report 36572). This boundary is irregular and non-contiguous, reflecting the actual limits of licensed or assigned survey blocks.

**Numeric annotations** are densely distributed throughout all blocks. These are **report/work numbers** (номера отчётов или работ) — referencing previous geological reports filed in the Kazakhstani geological fund. Examples visible include:
- Upper area: 100, 239, 350, 402, 2204, 301, 351, 408, 403, 316, 85, 381
- Middle area: 676, 260, 1075, 737, 814, 097, 409, 2001, 828, 1076, 248-254, 372, 240-251, 55-4, 571, 605, 507, 608
- Lower area: 333, 700, 829, 2001, 2800, 700, 2002, 1600, 238, 555, 561, 3000, 240, 838, 345, 235-237, 1020, 864, 858, 1501, 1502, 1503, 783, 810, 179, 544, 1593, 758, 038, 831, 561, 838, and many others

These numbers correspond to archived reports that previously covered each sub-area, allowing assessment of **prior geological knowledge density**.

The coverage is **highly variable**: some blocks (particularly in the upper/northern portion — sheets XXXII–XXXV) show extensive prior coverage with many report numbers, while the southwestern blocks (I, VII, XIII) appear to have fewer annotations, suggesting less prior coverage.

---
